title: simsia subsetosa
intro: Simsia subsetosa, also known as the yellow flowers of the West Indies, is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antiparasitic effects. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health issues, such as fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, and has been shown to have antimicrobial and antioxidant properties, offering potential health benefits. Simsia subsetosa is typically prepared as an infusion, tincture, or powder for use in herbal remedies.
